mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present value of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over a 5-year rolling window, or positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 50 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, a sustained decline in the industrial production index reflects capacity constraints and slowing real output, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to predict negative returns when applied to early-cycle or recession regimes, or the 60-day forward return correlation with INDPRO declines is not significantly different from zero in late-cycle samples.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index crosses above 240.0, it indicates accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 70 percent of observations following the CPIAUCSL threshold crossing, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are already priced in.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and higher aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS levels remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive association with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while retail sales accelerate, it signals a shift toward consumption-driven growth that masks underlying economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series models, or 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances where the divergence condition holds.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index crosses above 102.0, it confirms a sustained recovery in manufacturing and capacity utilization, which boosts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive 5-day returns in less than 55 percent of triggered cases, or the predictive power vanishes when tested on data outside recovery phases.
